234.reason: [The lexer does not handle comments. When encountering "//", it should skip the rest of the line, but instead it treats '/' as a DIV operator and then processes the following characters (like "输出 1") as identifiers and constants, producing extra tokens.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The lexer does not handle comments. When encountering "//", it should skip the rest of the line, but instead it treats '/' as a DIV operator and then processes the following characters (like "输出 1") as identifiers and constants, producing extra tokens.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  The lexer does not check for comments starting with "//". When encountering '/', it should first check if the next character is also '/', and if so, skip the rest of the line instead of treating it as a DIV token.

89.reason: The code fails to handle single-line comments starting with '//'. It treats '/' as a division operator and the subsequent '/' as another division operator or part of an identifier, instead of skipping the comment content until the end of the line.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The code fails to handle single-line comments starting with '//'. It treats '/' as a division operator and the subsequent '/' as another division operator or part of an identifier, instead of skipping the comment content until the end of the line.

235.reason: [This treats every '/' as a DIV token without checking for comments; in the test case, "// 输出 1" is parsed as two DIV tokens followed by an identifier and integer, but should be treated as a comment and skipped entirely.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [This treats every '/' as a DIV token without checking for comments; in the test case, "// 输出 1" is parsed as two DIV tokens followed by an identifier and integer, but should be treated as a comment and skipped entirely.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [This handles '/' as DIV but fails to check for comment syntax '//' which should be skipped entirely during lexical analysis, not treated as two DIV tokens.
